title: Tooling Upgrade Pass — Skills-First Research Pipeline
summary: Today’s pass focused on operational upgrades: tighter search tooling, safer skill intake, and better job orchestration. The key shift is from one-off prompting to repeatable jobs with explicit ownership, review gates, and source discipline.

stats.text: Installed and vetted for search, browser automation, UX, SQL, TTS, and workflow automation.
VirusTotal gates correctly blocked risky installs until explicit approval.
`AGENT_JOBS.md` now defines consistent roles, lanes, approval gates, and done criteria.

items.headline: Jobs-first operating model wired into projects
items.note: Daily-AI and CuZn now have TASKS.json lanes and job ownership structure linked to AGENT_JOBS.md.

items.headline: ClawHub-driven shortlist + security vetting in production
items.note: Installed shortlist via CLI; blocked suspicious packages by default; allowed only after explicit human approval.

items.headline: Agent-browser approved with risk note
items.note: High-power browser automation added for trusted tasks only due to elevated session/control surface.

items.headline: OpenClaw: Mission Control + Agent Teams
items.note: Strong pattern: central task board + live feed + approval gates for multi-agent reliability.

items.headline: OpenClaw Skills Tutorial
items.note: High signal on building reusable local skills and reducing prompt drift with explicit workflow docs.

items.headline: How to create JOBS for OpenClaw agents
items.note: Best takeaway: treat agents like employees with clear job definitions, not ad-hoc prompts.

items.headline: Research stack expansion
items.note: tavily-search, multi-search-engine, youtube-watcher, web-search-plus improve coverage and fallback behavior.

items.headline: Execution stack expansion
items.note: playwright-mcp + agent-browser + automation-workflows add deeper automation options.

items.headline: Data and reporting stack
items.note: sql-toolkit and excel-xlsx support better structured outputs for dashboards and operational reporting.

implementation: Next step is to enforce one concrete daily job pack: (1) source harvest, (2) claim verification, (3) synthesis, (4) publish. Each stage gets an owner and done definition. This should materially improve consistency and reduce noisy updates.

title: Daily AI Update — Shipping-Grade Agents, China Model Scale, and Local-First Execution
summary: Today’s signal is clear: AI stacks are maturing from demo outputs into reliability infrastructure. US labs are publishing control/eval primitives, China players are racing model scale and deployment, and open-source momentum remains strongest where teams can run workflows locally and audit behavior end-to-end.

stats.text: Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max reported at trillion-plus scale, reinforcing that frontier competition now spans both capability and deployment economics.
Promptfoo’s previously disclosed enterprise penetration is a strong indicator that eval/security gates are now standard procurement requirements.
Today’s hot GitHub list clusters around agent orchestration, memory, and production workflow glue rather than pure model demos.

items.headline: China frontier scale keeps accelerating
items.note: Reuters reports Qwen3-Max above the trillion-parameter mark, which keeps pressure on cost-efficient training and inference optimization across the ecosystem.

items.headline: Enterprise eval is now mandatory infra
items.note: OpenAI’s Promptfoo move highlights that red-teaming and regression testing are no longer optional add-ons; they are shipping gates for customer trust.

items.headline: Agent workflow repos continue compounding
items.note: OpenHands and adjacent projects keep gaining adoption because they solve practical loops (plan → act → verify), not just prompt quality.

items.headline: OpenAI launches Instruction Hierarchy Challenge
items.note: This pushes agent safety toward measurable behavior under conflicting directives. Why it matters: more robust instruction prioritization directly reduces tool misuse in autonomous workflows.

items.headline: OpenAI expands education push inside ChatGPT
items.note: Product direction emphasizes guided learning flows over single-shot answers, signaling broader adoption of tutor-style interaction patterns.

items.headline: Google reports SOTA gains for Gemini in Sheets
items.note: Google is moving AI value deeper into everyday productivity surfaces. Why it matters: spreadsheet-native AI is becoming a mainstream ops interface.

items.headline: Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max raises China frontier bar
items.note: Corroboration: Reuters coverage plus Alibaba/Qwen channels show continued high-velocity model competition in China and stronger domestic ecosystem pull.

items.headline: DeepSeek reportedly trained on Nvidia H100 despite controls
items.note: If sustained, this suggests compute supply constraints are reshaping logistics and policy strategy as much as model architecture strategy.

items.headline: OpenHands/OpenHands
items.note: Open-source software engineer agent stack with practical issue-to-PR flow design worth borrowing for task decomposition and verification.

items.headline: microsoft/semantic-kernel
items.note: Still one of the cleanest abstractions for memory, tool wiring, and multi-provider orchestration in production applications.

items.headline: FlowiseAI/Flowise
items.note: Low-friction way to prototype RAG and agent pipelines visually before hardening them in code.

items.headline: Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT
items.note: Large community gravity remains; useful benchmark for long-running agent task orchestration patterns.

items.headline: OpenHands/OpenHands
items.note: Rapidly growing implementation reference for coding-agent loops with observable state transitions.

items.headline: FlowiseAI/Flowise
items.note: Strong adoption as teams test and share repeatable agent/RAG templates.

items.headline: thedotmack/claude-mem
items.note: Memory layer patterns are increasingly central as assistants shift from one-shot to ongoing operator roles.

items.headline: microsoft/semantic-kernel
items.note: Enterprise-oriented orchestration SDK with durable momentum across .NET/Python ecosystems.

items.headline: simstudioai/sim
items.note: No-code/low-code automation angle keeps attracting product teams that need fast iteration before full engineering investment.

items.headline: OpenHands pilot on one internal repo
items.note: Run issue triage + fix proposals locally first; compare acceptance quality vs your current manual review flow.

items.headline: Flowise local RAG prototype for Daily-AI knowledge base
items.note: Create a local retriever over prior posts to suggest non-redundant angles automatically each day.

items.headline: Semantic Kernel local tool-routing sandbox
items.note: Test deterministic tool-calling with stricter function contracts before exposing any external side effects.

items.headline: OpenAI + Promptfoo direction
items.note: Use eval suites as CI gates: prompt-injection, leakage, refusal consistency, and tool permission boundaries.

items.headline: Google Gemini for Workspace APIs
items.note: Practical for spreadsheet-centric operations where analysts need AI in-place rather than in separate chat interfaces.

items.headline: Hugging Face storage + model distribution stack
items.note: Helpful for teams that need controlled artifact management while iterating on open-source model workflows.

items.headline: Set a Daily-AI source confidence rubric
items.note: Add per-item labels (primary source, secondary corroboration, confidence score) so quality stays stable as volume grows.

items.headline: Build an eval gate before publish
items.note: Auto-check every draft for section completeness, stale links, duplicate headlines, and missing why-it-matters context.

items.headline: Prototype China/US balanced coverage alerts
items.note: Run a daily fetch that enforces regional balance so the digest doesn’t drift into single-ecosystem bias.

implementation: 1) Add a pre-publish validator script that fails if required sections/counts are missing. 2) Add dual-link requirement (primary + corroboration) for every Breaking AI item. 3) Add a lightweight trend tracker over last 7 posts so repeated topics get deprioritized automatically.

title: Enhanced with Playwright Deep Scraping
summary: First run with full article extraction. BBC lawsuit story pulled with actual quotes. HN top stories scraped with live links. Deep content now achievable.

items.headline: Anthropic sues US government for calling it a risk
items.note: AI firm Anthropic filed a first-of-its-kind lawsuit against the US government over 'supply chain risk' label. CEO Dario Amodei publicly clashed with Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth over the company's refusal to allow the military unfettered use of its AI tools.

items.headline: Anthropic launches code review tool for AI-generated code
items.note: Claude Code has dramatically increased code output, creating PR review bottlenecks. Anthropic's new Code Review tool targets this directly.

items.headline: I put my whole life into a single database
items.note: Personal data engineering project going viral on HN

items.headline: Redox OS adopts Certificate of Origin + strict no-LLM policy
items.note: Interesting stance on LLM contributions in OS development

items.headline: Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed
items.note: Major European AI funding milestone

items.headline: Two Years of Emacs Solo
items.note: Personal editor journey going strong

implementation: Playwright scraping working. Next: extract full article text for key stories to give actual context in digest, not just headlines.

title: AI News Digest (Baseline Format)
summary: Today's AI cycle is all about production hardening: security buys, multi-agent code review, robotics-first edge hardware, and the growing split between local-first and API-first tooling.

stats.text: weekly stars on ruvnet/RuView, signaling demand for sensor-based AI beyond camera pipelines.
NPU in Qualcomm's new Ventuno Q board, pushing embedded AI robotics into maker and industrial workflows.
already use Promptfoo, the evaluation/security tooling OpenAI is acquiring to tighten enterprise AI app safety.

items.headline: OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
items.note: OpenAI's move suggests eval and security tooling is becoming core product infrastructure, not an add-on.

items.headline: Anthropic ships multi-agent Code Review in Claude Code preview
items.note: A strong signal that AI coding assistants are moving from generation into quality gates and review automation.

items.headline: Qualcomm + Arduino launch Ventuno Q for AI robotics
items.note: Ventuno Q combines 16GB RAM with a 40 TOPS NPU to target autonomous robotics and sensor-driven edge workloads.

items.headline: Microsoft tests Claude Cowork integration in Copilot Frontier
items.note: Enterprise copilots may increasingly become orchestration shells over multiple model providers.

items.headline: Kapwing publishes artist royalty learnings for AI-generated art
items.note: One of the cleaner public case studies on compensation models for generative media businesses.

items.headline: 666ghj/MiroFish
items.note: A swarm-intelligence engine for broad prediction workflows and alternative AI experimentation.

items.headline: DenchHQ/DenchClaw
items.note: A local CRM layer built on OpenClaw, useful reference for verticalized autonomous workflows.

items.headline: strands-agents/samples
items.note: Composable agent patterns and workflow examples for orchestration benchmarking.

items.headline: 666ghj/MiroFish
items.note: +5,022/week — strong momentum for non-transformer inference patterns.

items.headline: virattt/ai-hedge-fund
items.note: Agentic finance experimentation repo with simulation pipeline ideas.

items.headline: strands-agents/samples
items.note: Practical examples for multi-agent execution and handoffs.

items.headline: exo-explore/exo
items.note: Distributed/local compute experimentation for running AI workloads across devices.

items.headline: Blaizzy/mlx-audio
items.note: Apple-silicon focused audio AI tooling for local speech/music workflows.

items.headline: alibaba/page-agent
items.note: Agent framework focused on web/page action patterns with automation potential.

items.headline: Ventuno Q class edge builds
items.note: Prototype sensor+actuation loops where inference stays on-device for latency/privacy.

items.headline: MiroFish + local data
items.note: Test swarm-based predictors against operational metrics before transformer-heavy stacks.

items.headline: DenchClaw-style vertical agents
items.note: Build domain-specific assistants directly on existing OpenClaw workflows.

items.headline: Promptfoo ecosystem
items.note: Eval/security now looks like procurement-grade infrastructure.

items.headline: Copilot Frontier + Cowork integration
items.note: Likely template for enterprise multi-provider orchestration.

items.headline: Claude Code Review preview
items.note: Early signal of review-as-a-service for CI/CD pipelines.

implementation: 1) Pilot Promptfoo-style security gate in one repo with explicit fail conditions for prompt-injection, leakage, and unsafe tool calls. 2) Run side-by-side benchmark (human-only vs multi-agent code review) on a recent PR batch and measure defect yield/time. 3) Prototype one local edge agent loop (sensor/event -> local model -> action) to validate latency and reliability assumptions before scaling.
